relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job lifted his discourse again.
He longed for the old months when the Divine guarded him.
A lamp once shone over his head, and light guided him through darkness.
Those were days of ripeness, when divine counsel rested over his tent.
Shaddai felt near, and his children surrounded him.
Abundance seemed to flow like butter and oil from stone.
He remembered honor at the city gate.
Young men withdrew and elders stood.
Princes held their words.
Nobles fell silent.
Those who heard and saw bore witness to him.
He had rescued the poor and the orphan without helper.
The dying blessed him, and widows found joy through him.
Righteousness and justice clothed him like garments.
He became eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
He fathered the needy and searched out unknown cases.
He broke predatory injustice and freed the prey.
He thought his days would multiply in peace.
His root touched water, and dew rested on his branch.
His glory stayed fresh and his strength renewed.
People waited for his counsel.
His words settled over them.
They waited for him like rain.
His face gave courage where confidence was gone.
He sat like a leader and comforter among mourners.
